title: 10 best herbal creams for loss of appetite

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with loss of appetite because they contain a unique combination of herbal extracts that work together to stimulate digestive health. The creams help to soothe and calm the stomach, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with decreased appetite. Additionally, they promote the release of digestive enzymes, which can improve nutrient absorption and satiety, helping individuals feel more satisfied and less inclined to lose interest in food.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Adiantum capillus-veneris may help reduce inflammation in the body, which could potentially alleviate symptoms of anorexia or loss of appetite.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant compounds present in Adiantum capillus-veneris may help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage, which could contribute to improved overall health and potentially increase appetite.

Stomach-soothing properties: Some traditional remedies suggest that Adiantum capillus-veneris can help soothe digestive issues like nausea, bloating, or stomach pain, which are common symptoms of loss of appetite.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maidenhair tree contain bioactive compounds that help stimulate appetite and improve digestion, making them a valuable component in medicinal creams.
Barks: The barks of the maidenhair tree possess anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms associated with loss of appetite.
remedy_recipe: Gather ingredients including 100g ginkgo biloba leaves, 50g coconut oil, and 20g beeswax, measuring cups, and a double boiler.
Dry ginkgo biloba leaves in a low-temperature oven at 60°c for 30 minutes to remove excess moisture and preserve active compounds.
Combine 50g dried ginkgo biloba leaves with 100ml carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a heat-resistant glass container, steeping for 2 hours under the sun.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the liquid from the solids, retaining 20ml of the infused oil and discarding the solids.

Mix the strained oil with 50g melted coconut oil, 10g melted beeswax, and 2% vitamin e oil in a double boiler, heating until the mixture reaches a temperature of 80°c for 30 minutes to create a smooth and consistent cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with loss of appetite because they stimulate the digestive system, increasing saliva production and secretion of digestive enzymes. This can help alleviate feelings of nausea and discomfort that often accompany a loss of appetite. Additionally, peppermint's calming effects on the mind and body can reduce stress and anxiety, which are common underlying causes of decreased appetite. By promoting digestive health and relaxation, herbal peppermint creams can help restore an individual's natural desire to eat and nourish their body.
remedy_properties: Digestive soothing compounds: Peppermint cream contains digestive soothing compounds like menthol, menthone, and limonene, which can help to ease digestive discomfort and reduce nausea, making it easier to consume food and regain appetite.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory properties in peppermint cream may help to reduce inflammation in the gut, which can contribute to loss of appetite by alleviating symptoms like bloating, cramping, and pain.

Menthol's stimulation of digestion: Menthol in peppermint cream can stimulate digestive enzymes and increase gastric motility, helping to improve digestion and reduce feelings of fullness or discomfort that can lead to a decrease in appetite.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the peppermint plant contain menthol, which has a calming effect on the stomach and can help stimulate appetite.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes of peppermint plant have antispasmodic properties that can help soothe digestive issues associated with loss of appetite.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves in the morning for maximum potency.
Dry the peppermint leaves by spreading them out in a single layer on paper towels for 7-10 days.
Grind the dried peppermint leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix the ground peppermint powder with beeswax and coconut oil at a ratio of 1:2:1 to create a smooth cream base that takes approximately 15 minutes.
Fill clean glass jars with the herbal cream mixture and store them in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm creams helps with loss of appetite because it soothes and protects the mucous membranes lining the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and irritation that can lead to a lack of interest in food. Its anti-inflammatory properties also help to calm nausea and bloating, making it easier for individuals to tolerate and digest meals. Additionally, slippery elm's gentle and non-irritating nature makes it an ideal ingredient for those experiencing stomach upset or digestive issues, helping to promote a healthier and more balanced appetite.
remedy_properties: Mucilage content: Slippery elm cream contains a thick, protective mucilage that coats the mucous membranes in the mouth and throat, soothing irritation and inflammation associated with decreased appetite, making it more comfortable to eat and drink.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The anti-inflammatory properties of slippery elm cream may help reduce discomfort or pain that can contribute to loss of appetite, allowing individuals to feel more inclined to consume food and drinks.

Sobering and demulcent properties: Slippery elm cream has a calming effect on the digestive system, which can help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with decreased appetite, making it easier for individuals to eat and digest their meals.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes digestive issues and can help stimulate appetite.
Barks: Contain compounds like salicin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that may aid in reducing inflammation associated with loss of appetite.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ulmus rubra leaves measuring approximately 100 grams for subsequent processing.
Dry the harvested leaves using a warm air circulation method for 7-10 hours to reduce moisture content.
Combine one part dried ulmus rubra leaves with two parts olive oil in a double boiler heating mixture at moderate heat.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes, then strain and filter the resulting herbal extract through a cheesecloth to remove solids.
Blend the filtered herbal extract with 20% beeswax and 10% coconut oil, melting them together at temperatures below 120 degrees fahrenheit for 30-45 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with loss of appetite because they stimulate digestion and increase the sensation of fullness, making it easier to eat. The warm, soothing properties of ginger also help to calm nausea and discomfort, reducing feelings of anxiety and stress that often accompany a lack of appetite. Additionally, the aromatic compounds in ginger have been shown to enhance flavor and aroma, making food more appealing and enjoyable. As a result, herbal ginger creams can be a natural and effective way to increase appetite and improve overall eating habits.
remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulants: Ginger's natural digestive stimulant properties can help increase appetite by improving digestion and reducing nausea, allowing for better nutrient absorption and increased hunger.

Inflammation Reduction: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, may help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to loss of appetite, and promote a healthier gut microbiome.

Antiemetic Properties: Ginger's natural antispasmodic and antiemetic properties can help alleviate symptoms of nausea and vomiting associated with appetite loss, making it easier for individuals to eat and digest food.
remedy_parts: Roots: Ginger roots have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to stimulate appetite and improve digestion, making them a popular choice for treating loss of appetite.

Rhyzomes: The rhizome of the ginger plant is rich in bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with loss of appetite, such as nausea and vomiting.
remedy_recipe: Collect dried ginger root and grind it into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground ginger powder with sweet almond oil in a small saucepan and heat gently over medium heat for 30 minutes.
Add vitamin e oil and beeswax to the mixture and stir until the wax is fully incorporated and melted.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool slightly before whipping the mixture with an electric mixer until it becomes creamy and smooth.
Pour the herbal cream into glass jars and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight before using to help stimulate appetite and digestive health.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with loss of appetite because they contain essential oils that stimulate digestion and relieve bloating. The anti-inflammatory properties in fennel oil also help to soothe the digestive system, reducing discomfort and nausea that can be a barrier to eating. Additionally, fennel's sweet anise flavor is often calming to the palate, making it easier for individuals with decreased appetite to eat and enjoy their meals.
remedy_properties: Carminative property: Fennel cream's carminative property helps to relieve digestive discomfort and reduce bloating, which can be a common cause of loss of appetite.

Anxiolytic property: The anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties of fennel cream help to calm the mind and reduce stress, making it easier for individuals with appetite loss to feel like eating again.
Gastric stimulant property: Fennel cream's gastric stimulant property helps to increase digestive juices and improve digestion, which can stimulate appetite and make food more appealing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots have been traditionally used to stimulate appetite and improve digestion, making them a good choice for medicinal creams.
Seeds: Fennel seeds have a carminative effect, which helps to reduce gas and bloating, and can be used in medicinal creams to alleviate digestive discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Gather and dry 100 grams of foeniculum vulgare leaves and stems for use in the cream.
Mix 20 grams of dried foeniculum vulgare with 100 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture for 30 minutes, then let it steep for an additional 60 minutes before straining.
Add 10 grams of beeswax and 5 grams of vitamin e oil to the cooled liquid and stir until melted.
Pour the final cream into sterilized jars, seal tightly, and store in a cool dark place for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with loss of appetite because they contain curcumin, a powerful compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Curcumin has been shown to stimulate appetite and improve digestion by reducing inflammation in the stomach and intestines. It also increases the production of gastric juices, which helps to break down food more efficiently. Additionally, turmeric creams may help to reduce stress and anxiety, common causes of loss of appetite, by promoting relaxation and reducing cortisol levels. As a result, using herbal turmeric creams can be an effective way to increase appetite and improve overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Curcuminoids: Curcuminoids, particularly curcumin, have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce inflammation in the gut, which can contribute to a lack of appetite.

Cuminum Cyminum Oil (Cumin Seed Oil): The essential fatty acids present in cumin seed oil help to improve digestion and alleviate symptoms of indigestion, bloating, and discomfort that can lead to loss of appetite.

Ginger Extract: Ginger has natural anti-inflammatory compounds called gingerols and shogaols that can help to reduce nausea, vomiting, and other digestive issues that may be contributing to a lack of appetite.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in curcumin, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that helps stimulate appetite and improve digestion.
Barks: Contains volatile oils that can help increase gastric motility and appetite stimulation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried turmeric roots curcuma longa and grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix the ground turmeric powder with 100g of cold-pressed coconut oil in a heat-resistant glass bowl for 5 minutes.
Add 20g of beeswax pellets to the mixture and stir until they are fully dissolved over low heat in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Combine 50g of shea butter and 50g of vitamin e oil with the turmeric-coconut oil mixture and blend until smooth using an immersion blender for 3 minutes.
Pour the mixture into glass jars and store them in the refrigerator at 4°c to set for at least 2 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with loss of appetite because its calming properties can soothe the digestive system, reducing feelings of anxiety and stress that often contribute to a lack of appetite. The cream's natural scent can also stimulate the appetite center in the brain, increasing hunger and desire for food. Additionally, lavender has anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other digestive disorders that can cause loss of appetite.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (anti-anxiety) effects: Lavender's calming and soothing properties can reduce anxiety and stress-related symptoms, which are common underlying causes of decreased appetite.

Gastrointestinal soothing properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties can help to soothe digestive issues such as nausea, indigestion, and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), which can contribute to loss of appetite.

Mood-boosting compounds: Lavender contains linalool and linalyl acetate, which are believed to have a positive effect on mood, reducing feelings of depression, anxiety, and stress that may be contributing to loss of appetite.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lavender roots contain a high concentration of apigenin, a flavonoid that has been shown to have anxiolytic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of loss of appetite.

Leaves: The leaves of the lavender plant contain a compound called linalool, which is known for its calming and relaxing effects, making it useful in soothing digestive issues associated with loss of appetite.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers at dawn and dry them immediately using air circulation or desiccation for 7-10 days.
Grind dried lavender flowers into fine powder using mortar and pestle, yielding approximately 20 grams per cup.
Combine 200ml of cooled boiled water with 1/4 cup ground lavender powder and steep for 10 minutes under warm sunlight or gentle heat.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter, squeezing gently to extract essential oils and then discard solids.
Mix the infused liquid with 100g of unscented cocoa butter, 20g of beeswax, and 10g of coconut oil, whipping until smooth and creamy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with loss of appetite because they have a calming effect on the digestive system, soothing any inflammation or discomfort that may be causing lack of hunger. The anti-inflammatory properties in chamomile also help to reduce anxiety and stress, common triggers for reduced appetite. Additionally, the cream's gentle, non-irritating nature can ease digestive issues such as nausea, making it easier to consume food and increase overall appetite. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms such as bloating and discomfort that may be contributing to a lack of appetite.

Anxiolytic property: Chamomile has anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effects, which can help to calm the nervous system and alleviate feelings of anxiety or stress that may be causing a decrease in appetite.

Musculotropic property: Chamomile's musculotropic property helps to relax smooth muscle tissue in the digestive tract, promoting easier digestion and absorption of nutrients, which can increase appetite and improve overall nutrient intake.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of chamomile contain apigenin, an antioxidant that can help stimulate appetite and reduce nausea.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers have been traditionally used to soothe digestive issues and promote relaxation, making them suitable for treating loss of appetite.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in sufficient quantity for the desired amount of cream.
Mix one teaspoon of dried flowers with two tablespoons of boiling water and let it steep for 10 minutes to create a strong infusion.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl, squeezing the solids gently to extract as much essence as possible.
Combine the extracted essence with one cup of petroleum jelly and four tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler, heating the mixture over low heat until it reaches 180°f for 15 minutes.
Remove the cream from heat, let it cool slightly, then transfer it into sterilized jars and store them in the refrigerator at a consistent temperature below 40°f for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with loss of appetite because they contain the natural herb, sage, which has been shown to stimulate appetite and improve digestion. remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe digestive issues and stimulate appetite.
Barks: The barks of the sage plant contain flavonoids, which can help reduce inflammation in the gut and alleviate symptoms of loss of appetite.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried salvia officinalis leaves and crush them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered leaves with 50 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat for 3 hours at low temperature.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it steep for 24 hours to allow for maximum extraction of active compounds.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding the solids and retaining the infused oil.
Mix the infused oil with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of vitamin e oil in a small saucepan over low heat until the mixture reaches a smooth consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with loss of appetite because they possess natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe digestive issues, which are often a primary cause of decreased appetite. The cream's gentle application to the skin allows for easy absorption into the bloodstream, where it can work to calm upset stomachs and alleviate feelings of nausea. remedy_properties: Thymol: Thymol has antimicrobial and antispasmodic properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and nausea, making it easier for individuals with loss of appetite to eat.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid is an antioxidant that helps to protect the mucous membranes in the stomach and intestines from damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and irritation that may contribute to loss of appetite.

Carvacrol: Carvacrol has antiviral and antibacterial properties, which can help to combat infections such as gastroenteritis that may cause loss of appetite, as well as reduce symptoms of indigestion and bloating.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots are used because they contain a high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which can help stimulate appetite and improve digestion.
Buds: Thyme buds are used due to their rich source of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to enhance appetite and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and wash them gently under running water.
Combine the harvested thymus vulgaris leaves with 100 milliliters of vegetable oil in a clean glass jar.
Place the mixture in a dark cupboard for 14 days to allow for infusing and maceration.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid infusion.
Mix the resulting infused oil with 200 grams of beeswax and 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil in a double boiler, heating until melted and blended.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary creams helps with loss of appetite because they contain natural compounds that stimulate digestion and improve nutrient absorption. The essential oils present in rosemary have been shown to increase gastric motility, reducing symptoms of nausea and vomiting, which can be common causes of decreased appetite. Improves cognitive function: Rosemary's rosmarinic acid has been shown to enhance memory and concentration, which may help individuals with decreased appetite due to depression, anxiety, or other mental health conditions regain interest in food.

Leaves: The leaves of the rosemary plant contain essential oils that help to improve digestion, reduce nausea, and increase appetite.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves and rinse them gently under cold running water for 30 seconds.
Combine 100g of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves with 500ml of boiling distilled water and steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bowl, discarding the solids, and let it cool to room temperature within 60 minutes.
Mix 50g of beeswax, 20g of coconut oil, and 30ml of the cooled herbal infusion in a double boiler, heating until melted and smooth after 10 minutes.
Pour the warm cream mixture into sterilized glass jars and seal tightly, allowing it to cool and set at room temperature for 2 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass creams helps with loss of appetite because they have a unique ability to stimulate digestion and improve overall gut health. remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality cymbopogon citratus leaves in the early morning or evening, ensuring optimal essential oil content.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 24 hours or by air drying for 3-5 days.
Grind 20g of dried cymbopogon citratus leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder and mix with 10g of coconut oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 40°c for 30 minutes, allowing the essential oils to infuse into the coconut oil.
Strain the infusion through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and transfer to sterilized containers, storing in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with loss of appetite because they stimulate digestion and improve nutrient absorption, which can help to increase hunger and interest in food. remedy_recipe: Harvest dried taraxacum officinale flowers in adequate amounts for the recipe.
Grind 30g of dried taraxacum officinale flowers into a fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Mix 10ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil with 5g of powdered taraxacum officinale flowers.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 20 minutes to allow for infusion and solvent extraction.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and storing it in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginseng creams helps with loss of appetite because they stimulate the brain's appetite center, increasing desire for food. remedy_recipe: Grind 20 grams of dried panax ginseng root into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground ginseng powder with 10 milliliters of sweet almond oil and 5 milliliters of coconut oil in a double boiler setup.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the oils have absorbed the medicinal properties of the ginseng.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to room temperature before mixing in 2 teaspoons of beeswax and 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil.
Pour the final mixture into a sterilized glass jar and store it in a cool, dark place for at least 24 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with loss of appetite because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has been traditionally used to stimulate appetite and improve digestion. remedy_recipe: Collect and dry 20 grams of dried licorice root extract and grind it into fine powder.
Mix the powdered licorice root extract with 50 grams of beeswax and 30 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to around 60 degrees celsius before whipping it with an electric whisk for 5 minutes.
Pour the whipped cream into sterile jars and store them in a cool place for at least 2 weeks before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Borage creams helps with loss of appetite because they contain the herb Calendula, which has been traditionally used to stimulate appetite and improve digestion. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh borago officinalis leaves in abundance and rinse them thoroughly with cool water.
Combine one cup of the harvested leaves with two cups of cold-pressed olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a dark cabinet at room temperature for four to six weeks, allowing it to macerate slowly.
After the maceration period, strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container, discarding the solids.
Mix one tablespoon of the herbal infused oil with three tablespoons of beeswax and heat gently over low heat until the wax melts and blends smoothly with the oil, creating a creamy consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with loss of appetite because they contain a high concentration of flavonoids, which have been shown to stimulate appetite and improve digestion. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers in the morning when they are at their peak potency.
Dry the harvested flowers using a food dehydrator or air drying method for 7-10 days.
Grind 20g of dried calendula flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder.
Mix 100ml of sweet almond oil with 5ml of beeswax and 2g of the ground calendula powder in a double boiler setup.
Heat the mixture for 15-20 minutes while stirring occasionally until the beeswax is fully melted and the cream has cooled to room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow creams helps with loss of appetite because they soothe and calm the digestive system, alleviating discomfort and inflammation that can lead to a decrease in food intake. The anti-inflammatory properties of marshmallows also help reduce irritation in the mouth, throat, and esophagus, making eating less painful and more enjoyable. Additionally, marshmallow creams can aid in the digestion of food, allowing the body to absorb nutrients more effectively, which can further increase appetite and improve overall nutrition.
remedy_properties: Soothing Mucilage: The mucilages present in marshmallow creams coat the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and irritation, which can contribute to loss of appetite by making eating uncomfortable.

Demulcent Properties: Marshmallow creams' demulcent properties create a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, soothing digestive issues such as gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD), heartburn, and inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), which can lead to loss of appetite.

Anti-Inflammatory Compounds: Marshmallow creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and abdominal pain that can contribute to loss of appetite.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the marshmallow plant contain mucilage, which helps to soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with loss of appetite.
Seeds: Marshmallow seeds have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including loss of appetite, as they are rich in fiber and omega-3 fatty acids that help regulate bowel movements.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried marshmallow root althaea officinalis and grind into fine powder.
Combine the ground marshmallow root with 100ml of warm distilled water in a heatproof cup.
Steep the mixture for 15 minutes, then strain through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container.
Mix 30g of beeswax pellets, 20g of coconut oil and 10g of vitamin e oil with the herbal infusion.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and let it cool to form a solid cream that can be stored for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian creams helps with loss of appetite because they contain the natural herb valerian root, which has a calming effect on the digestive system. The cream can soothe the stomach and intestines, reducing feelings of discomfort and indigestion that often lead to a decrease in appetite. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, valerian creams can help regulate appetite and improve overall digestion, making it easier to eat and enjoy meals again. This natural approach can be especially helpful for individuals with stress-related eating disorders or those experiencing digestive issues.
remedy_properties: Sedative and calming effects: Valerian's sedative properties can help alleviate stress, anxiety, and restlessness, which are common underlying causes of loss of appetite.

Digestive soothing properties: Valerian has been traditionally used to calm digestive issues such as nausea, bloating, and indigestion, making it easier for individuals with reduced appetite to eat and digest food properly.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: Valerian's antioxidant properties can help reduce inflammation in the body, which is often linked to loss of appetite due to chronic illness or injury, allowing the individual to recover and regain their appetite naturally.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots are a rich source of valerenic acid, a compound that helps to stimulate appetite and reduce feelings of nausea.
Rhyzomes: Valerian rhyzomes contain sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to have anti-anorectic properties, helping to increase food intake in individuals with loss of appetite.
remedy_recipe: Harvest valeriana officinalis roots in mid-summer when they are mature and dry them immediately.
Grind 250g of dried valeriana officinalis roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 100g of the powdered valeriana officinalis with 200ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it reaches a temperature of 60°c.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify at room temperature before transferring it to a clean glass jar for storage.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for loss of appetite are topical preparations that combine natural herbs and ingredients to stimulate appetite and improve overall nutritional intake. These creams work by increasing the senses of smell and taste, which can be dulled by certain medical conditions or medications, leading to a decrease in appetite. Examples of herbal creams that help with loss of appetite include those containing ginger, peppermint, and lemongrass, which have been shown to increase salivation and improve digestion. By using these creams, individuals struggling with loss of appetite can regain their sense of taste and smell, allowing them to enjoy meals again and improve their overall quality of life.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat loss of appetite with herbal creams is to focus on stimulating digestion and increasing hunger pangs. Look for creams containing ginger, peppermint, and fennel, which have natural properties that can help alleviate nausea and indigestion. Apply a small amount to the abdomen or wrists before meals to encourage appetite and aid in nutrient absorption. Additionally, consider combining herbal creams with stress-reducing teas and gentle exercise for optimal results.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to loss of appetite that are treated with herbal creams are digestive issues such as constipation, irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), and inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). Herbal creams containing ingredients like aloe vera, slippery elm, and marshmallow root can help soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing symptoms like bloating, cramps, and diarrhea. These creams may also contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce inflammation and promote healing in the gut.
